heading: Hot dry rock geothermal energy
label: 1028 Hot dry rock geothermal energy
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Energy Policy Act of 1992, and not as part of the Geothermal Steam Act of 1970 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Definition of enhanced geothermal systems
children.label: a Definition of enhanced geothermal systems
children.text: In this section, the term “enhanced geothermal systems” has the meaning given the term in .section 17191 of title 42

children.heading: USGS program
children.label: b USGS program
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior, acting through the United States Geological Survey, and in consultation with the Secretary of Energy, shall establish a cooperative Government-private sector program with respect to hot dry rock geothermal energy resources on public lands (as such term is defined in ) and lands managed by the Department of Agriculture, other than any such public or other lands that are withdrawn from geothermal leasing. Such program shall include, but shall not be limited to, activities to identify, select, and classify those areas throughout the United States that have a high potential for hot dry rock geothermal energy production and activities to develop and disseminate information regarding the utilization of such areas for hot dry rock energy production. Such information may include information regarding field test processes and techniques for assuring that hot dry rock geothermal energy development projects are developed in an economically feasible manner without adverse environmental consequences. Utilizing the information developed by the Secretary, together with information developed in connection with other related programs carried out by other Federal agencies, the Secretary, acting through the United States Geological Survey, may also enter into contracts and cooperative agreements with any public or private entity to provide assistance to any such entity to enable such entity to carry out additional projects with respect to the utilization of hot dry rock geothermal energy resources which will further the purposes of this section.section 1702(e) of title 43

children.heading: Update to geothermal resource assessment
children.label: c Update to geothermal resource assessment
A
children.text: improving the resolution of resource potential at systematic temperatures and depths, including temperatures and depths appropriate for power generation and direct use applications;

children.label: B
children.text: quantifying the total potential to coproduce geothermal energy and minerals;

children.label: C
children.text: incorporating data relevant to underground thermal energy storage and exchange, such as aquifer and soil properties; and

children.label: i
children.text: maps that indicate key subsurface parameters for electric and direct use resources; and

children.label: ii
children.text: risk maps for induced seismicity based on geologic, geographic, and operational parameters; and

to the maximum extent practicable, by coordinating with relevant State officials and institutions of higher education to expand geothermal assessments, including enhanced geothermal systems assessments, to include assessments for the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico and the States of Alaska and Hawaii.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section.
